prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
- French: Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n’importe quelle dispute n’aurait pu le faire.
- Japanese: 私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな口論よりも多くを物語っていた。
- Arabic: عبّر الصمت بيننا عن أكثر مما كان يمكن لأي جدال أن يعبّر عنه.

Notes:
- “Said more than” is rendered idiomatically (French: en disait plus; Japanese: 物語っていた; Arabic: عبّر عن) to avoid a literal “said.”
- The past counterfactual “could have” is made explicit in French (n’aurait pu le faire) and Arabic (كان يمكن … أن), but is left implicit and natural in Japanese.
- “Argument” is taken to mean a quarrel, hence dispute (Fr.), 口論 (Ja.), and جدال (Ar.).
